philosophy: Anchor businesses are hand-crafted with specific owners, staff, and hooks. Procedural fill generates remaining businesses from regional flavor tables. All businesses track finite resources. Names pull from name banks with regional weighting.

anchor_businesses.name: The Marchwarden's Rest
anchor_businesses.description: Two storeys, slate roof, the name painted above the door in letters that were once gold and are now a respectable brown. Eight private rooms upstairs, a common room that fits thirty comfortably and fifty uncomfortably. The bread is exceptional. Everything else is adequate. The building is slightly better maintained than the finances suggest it should be, which is because Win Crale fixes things before they become problems.

water.source: Well (eastern bank) — hauled daily by Win. Two barrels.

food_stores.current_level: Six weeks — adequate but Oswin is watching grain prices
sources: Direct from Dawnfield farm (reduced since Morra is in town)

firewood.current_level: Adequate until Harrowfrost — needs replenishment before then
firewood.source: Purchased from logging operation via Ashford caravan

ale.source: Brewer on eastern bank — Halvard Brewer, separate NPC

pricing.bath_water: 3 iron chits extra — Win hauls it

anchor_businesses.hollow_road_arrangement: Waystation use of the back storeroom. One room kept loosely available. In exchange: one piece of regional intelligence per month delivered to Maren. No questions asked about sealed bags.
hooks: Aldric Vorn is staying here — his presence unsettles Maren
Fenwick Grale (Hollow Road) is waiting for a late pickup
The common room is the primary information exchange point in western Durnward

anchor_businesses.name: Temple of the Undying Ember
anchor_businesses.location: Durnward — western bank, faces the bridge
anchor_businesses.description: Stone construction, older than anything else on the western bank. The sacred flame has burned in the nave continuously for over two hundred years according to temple records. The library is small but genuine. The kitchen feeds anyone who asks once per day without conditions.

food_stores.current_level: Substantial — Soren has been quietly building reserves

candles_and_oil.notes: The sacred flame runs on imported oil; Soren tracks this carefully

bedding.current_level: More than usual — Soren has added capacity for refuge use without announcement

services: Daily meal to anyone who asks — no conditions
Floor sleeping for travelers in genuine need — Soren's discretion
Library access — literacy required, Soren's approval required
hooks: The disc is in Soren's desk
The library has a gap where something was recently removed
Soren is waiting for a letter that hasn't come

anchor_businesses.name: The Bent Loom Guild — Durnward Office
anchor_businesses.owner: The Bent Loom Guild (Halvard Keld, local factor)

staff.name: Aldren Marsh (garrison guard captain)
staff.role: Security — knows the warehouse holds more than cloth

anchor_businesses.description: The largest private building on the eastern bank. River-access loading doors. Well-maintained. The office is prosperous. The warehouse holds textile goods, general merchandise, and in a locked section that appears on no manifest, correspondence and financial instruments of political significance.

resources.goods_inventory: Extensive and tracked. Keld knows the value of everything in this building.
resources.correspondence: Encrypted copies of politically sensitive letters — Keld's insurance

hooks: Keld is hiring a clerk — the successful candidate has access to sensitive correspondence
Neva Weld works here part-time — the connection to Torven Weld is not coincidental
Keld is deciding whether to switch his backing from Pelvar to Jorn

anchor_businesses.name: The Marchwarden's Hall
anchor_businesses.description: Stone, practical, elevated above the river for defensive advantage and administrative visibility. The public room handles petitions every morning. The working rooms handle everything else. The eastern staircase has been cordoned off for three months — a repair deferred twice.

resources.administrative: The most complete records of the Marches' legal, financial, and military situation
resources.food_stores: Well-maintained — the Hall could sustain itself for months if needed
resources.garrison_connection: Direct access to the adjacent garrison

anchor_businesses.access: Public petitions mornings only. Other access through appointment or relationship with staff.
hooks: The eastern staircase repair — entry point for Carpenter background
Clerk and administrative work available through Aldren Croft
The most politically sensitive location in the Marches

anchor_businesses.name: The Durnward Garrison
anchor_businesses.location: Durnward — eastern bank, adjacent to Hall
anchor_businesses.description: Functional, adequately maintained, under-staffed relative to regional requirements. The budget cut that released the Levy Soldier background character is recent and has not been replaced.

current_postings.job: Auxiliary patrol rider — east road to Ashford

hooks: Aldric Vorn's meetings with garrison soldiers — visible to perceptive players
Weld's note about Vorn's indirect threat has not been shared with the Marchwarden

anchor_businesses.name: Vorn's Metalwork
anchor_businesses.owner: Aldeth Vorn (the smith — not Aldric Vorn)
anchor_businesses.description: A working forge that smells like hot iron and coal and sounds like productive work from half a street away. The stall at market is the public face; the forge is the actual business. Quality work, fair prices, currently strained by iron supply costs.

iron_stock.current_level: Three weeks of work at current intake
iron_stock.source: Trade caravans — expensive since the mine closed

hooks: Hiring an apprentice — Blacksmith's Apprentice background spawn hook
Iron supply negotiation with Halvard Keld — she has not agreed

durnward_roll_1d6.5: Second inn (lower quality than Marchwarden's Rest)

kelds_crossing_roll_1d4.2: Basic lodging (not a real inn — a family renting a room)

owner_generation.method: Pull name from regional name bank (file 08). Age range by business type: established trades 35-60, newer operations 25-40.

financial_state_by_business_type.Small trades: Struggling to comfortable depending on roll

owner_generation.schedule: Pull from NPC schedule templates in file 08 by archetype

all_businesses_track: Primary stock (what they sell or use in their trade)
Fuel (wood or coal for heat-dependent trades)

restock_mechanics.market_towns: Restocked via trade caravans on roughly 10-day intervals — aligns with Marketday schedule
restock_mechanics.remote_settlements: Restocked less frequently — Ashford every 20-30 days, Keld's Crossing irregularly
restock_mechanics.winter_restocking: Caravan frequency drops in Sternveld and Deepwarden — settlements must carry larger buffer stocks or face shortages
restock_mechanics.mine_closure_impact: Iron stock across all businesses is currently at 60-70% normal levels and rising in cost — this is a simulation variable, not a fixed number
